goal: JSC-363 Codex Runtime Evidence Verifier Cockpit
intent: Add the smallest deterministic browser-evidence validation lane so captured browser artifacts can fail closed on missing screenshots, blank screenshots, missing required viewport coverage, missing console telemetry, and console-error policy violations.

problemSignal.summary: Browser evidence schema and capture script exist, but rendered evidence is not yet a routine gate with explicit failure classification.
problemSignal.failureMode: A frontend or user-visible change can appear validated while the rendered artifact is missing, blank, unscoped to viewports, or hides console errors.

in: Deep module for browser-evidence manifest validation
CLI wiring through the existing evidence verification surface
Schema-supported manifest fields for required viewport coverage, captured viewport evidence, console telemetry, console policy, and blank-screenshot policy
Regression tests for missing screenshot, blank screenshot, viewport coverage, and console policy failures
out: Full visual diffing or golden-image comparison
Merge-readiness, delivery-truth, or Judge/PM claim support

architecture.publicSurface: Existing evidence-verify command receives an explicit browser-evidence manifest validation option instead of treating generic file verification as browser evidence proof.
architecture.separation: The validator classifies browser artifact quality but remains separate from UI capture, delivery-truth, review-state, external-state, and merge-readiness authority.

manifestShape.screenshots: Each screenshot must include repo-contained path, viewportId, width, height, and optional description.
manifestShape.viewportCoverage: requiredViewportIds must be supplied by CLI/policy/contract or explicitly mirrored from a reviewed manifest field; captured screenshot viewportIds must cover every required viewport ID.
manifestShape.consoleTelemetry: consoleEvents must be present, even when empty, so telemetry omission is distinct from no observed console events.
manifestShape.consolePolicy: Default policy fails error-level console events. Warning-level handling is explicit and must not silently fail open.
manifestShape.blankScreenshotPolicy: Validator must use deterministic thresholds: minimum dimensions, minimum byte size, and PNG pixel-channel diversity above an explicit minimum unique-colour threshold.

cliContract.requiredWhen: The browser-evidence manifest option is mandatory only when this lane is invoked directly or when a future contract/policy explicitly requires it for UI-affecting changes.

acceptanceCriteria: A valid browser-evidence manifest with screenshots, viewport coverage, and no disallowed console errors passes.
A manifest referencing a missing screenshot fails with a machine-readable code.
A blank screenshot fails with a machine-readable code.
A manifest without required viewport coverage fails with a machine-readable code.
A manifest without consoleEvents fails with a machine-readable missing-telemetry code.
Console errors fail by default unless the manifest policy explicitly allows them.
The command returns non-zero when browser-evidence validation fails and JSON output includes the browser evidence errors.
validationPlan: pnpm vitest run src/lib/browser-evidence/*.test.ts src/commands/evidence-verify.test.ts src/lib/cli/command-registry.test.ts

changesApplied: Made required/captured viewport coverage explicit and prevented generic file verification from implying browser-policy proof.
Required consoleEvents telemetry and default-deny error policy.
Replaced non-assertive validation command with fixture-backed positive and negative command expectations.
Defined deterministic blank-screenshot criteria at the intent level.

expected: ARCHITECTURE.md deep-module map if a new module is added
CLI/docs references only if the public command contract changes beyond an option

reviewRequirements: Run improve-codebase-architecture, simplify, unslopify, he-code-review, and testing lenses after implementation.
Use adversarial-reviewer, agent-native-reviewer, and best-practices-researcher before marking the slice done.

circleciCredentialHandling.sessionObservation: The path can be a FIFO in this environment; do not blindly source it because it can hang without a writer.
circleciCredentialHandling.fallback: If the FIFO cannot be read safely, classify direct CircleCI API access as credential_surface_fifo_no_writer and use GitHub check rollup for current CircleCI status.
